things.name: Girona Cathedral
things.description: Magnificent Gothic cathedral with the world's widest Gothic nave at 23 metres, set atop a broad baroque staircase

things.name: Jewish Quarter (El Call)
things.description: One of the best-preserved medieval Jewish quarters in Europe, a labyrinth of narrow lanes with the Museum of Jewish History

things.name: City Walls of Girona
things.description: Walk the ancient Roman and medieval walls encircling the old town for spectacular views over terracotta rooftops and the Pyrenees

things.name: Onyar Riverside Houses
things.description: Iconic row of colourful medieval houses overhanging the Onyar River, one of the most photographed scenes in Catalonia

things.name: Museu d'Art de Girona
things.description: Fine arts museum in the medieval Episcopal Palace with Romanesque to contemporary art spanning twelve centuries of Catalan creativity

things.name: Arab Baths
things.description: Well-preserved 12th-century baths built in Romanesque style with a beautiful lantern dome over the central cold-water pool

things.name: Monastery of Sant Pere de Galligants
things.description: Exquisite 12th-century Romanesque Benedictine monastery with a carved cloister, now housing the Archaeological Museum of Catalonia

things.name: Pont de les Peixateries Velles
things.description: Distinctive red iron bridge designed by Gustave Eiffel spanning the Onyar River, now a beloved symbol of modern Girona

things.name: Rambla de la Llibertat
things.description: Girona's lively promenade along the Onyar lined with flower stalls, bookshops and terrace cafes perfect for people-watching

things.name: Costa Brava Coast
things.description: The stunning rocky coves and fishing villages of the Costa Brava are just 30 minutes from Girona's old town
